project.name: stem-ai-bio
project.full_name: STEM-AI: Sovereign Trust Evaluator for Medical AI Artifacts
project.purpose: Rubric-based trust evaluation framework for open-source bio/medical AI repositories. Produces reproducible trust tier classifications (T0-T4) via 3-stage protocol with fixed scoring.
project.canonical_statement: STEM-AI is a universal AI agent skill that audits open-source bio/medical AI repositories for clinical trustworthiness. It cross-verifies README claims, code reality, social media consistency, and governance maturity to produce a numeric trust score and tier classification (T0-T4). The AI maintainer role is to execute audits per the IMMUTABLE rubric spec, protect design invariants against drift, record failure modes as lessons, and evolve the protocol through versioned patches.

design_invariants.label: stage-order-fixed
design_invariants.statement: 3-stage evaluation order is fixed: Stage 1 (README Dissection) -> Stage 2 (Cross-Platform) -> Stage 3 (Code/Bio). No stage may be reordered or skipped.

design_invariants.label: weighted-formula-fixed
design_invariants.statement: Weighted average formula is fixed: S1 x 0.40, S2 x 0.20, S3 x 0.40. Weight values are IMMUTABLE.

design_invariants.label: tier-boundaries-fixed
design_invariants.statement: Tier boundaries: T0(0-39), T1(40-54), T2(55-69), T3(70-84), T4(85-100). Boundaries are IMMUTABLE.

design_invariants.label: t0-hard-floor
design_invariants.statement: T0_HARD_FLOOR: CA-DIRECT classification + zero code presence = T0 regardless of stage scores. No override. No exception.

design_invariants.label: risk-penalty-checklist
design_invariants.statement: Risk penalties RP1(-15), RP2(-10), RP3(-10) are applied before tier assignment. Skipping any applicable penalty is a scoring error.

design_invariants.label: na-redistribution
design_invariants.statement: When a stage returns N/A, its weight redistributes proportionally to remaining stages. N/A is never treated as 0.

design_invariants.label: cross-llm-consistency-target
design_invariants.statement: Cross-LLM consistency target: identical input, any LLM, score within +/-10 points. Rubric choices must preserve this target.

design_invariants.label: anti-abuse-controls
design_invariants.statement: Anti-abuse: max 10 repos/session, no bulk sweeps, no individual profiling, no competitor analysis. Public repos only.

design_invariants.label: mandatory-disclaimer-block
design_invariants.statement: Mandatory disclaimer block must appear verbatim in every audit output. Omission is a validation failure (CHECK 9).

design_invariants.label: self-validation-gate-19-checks
design_invariants.statement: 19 mandatory self-validation checks (CHECK 1-19) must pass before output. Any FAIL must be corrected before proceeding.

design_invariants.label: ca-flag-three-tier
design_invariants.statement: Clinical adjacency flag is 3-tier: CA-DIRECT (patient-facing), CA-INDIRECT (clinical data), CA-PLANNED (roadmap only). Each tier has distinct graduated penalties.

design_invariants.label: t4-pending-denominator-80
design_invariants.statement: T4 PENDING denominator is 80 (not 85). Stage 3 rubric normalized to /80 when T4 PENDING path is active.

design_invariants.label: author-domain-context-zero-score
design_invariants.statement: Author domain context is informational only. Zero score impact. Domain background must not adjust, override, or reinterpret any score.

design_invariants.label: derived-1-expiry-formula
design_invariants.statement: DERIVED-1: expiry_date = last_commit_date + 365 days. Formula is fixed.

design_invariants.label: derived-2-audit-branch
design_invariants.statement: DERIVED-2: audit_branch = branch at audit time. Must be recorded in audit metadata.

design_invariants.label: derived-3-before-stage3
design_invariants.statement: DERIVED-3 (trajectory_signal) must be computed BEFORE Stage 3 execution (Section 8.2 Step 5a). Computing it after Stage 3 is an ordering violation.

design_invariants.label: governance-overlay-separation
design_invariants.statement: Stage 3G governance overlay is advisory only. Base tier is never modified by overlay score. G3=0 caps overlay verdict at WEAK regardless of other G scores.

design_invariants.label: immutable-rules-require-minor-version
design_invariants.statement: IMMUTABLE rules require a minor version increment (1.x.0) to change. Patch versions (1.x.x) may not modify IMMUTABLE rules.

discrimination_examples.description: YES/NO calibration examples for scoring boundary cases

invoke_role_semantics.note: IMMUTABLE rubric items are anchor-pinned and never evicted from context.

anchor.meaning: IMMUTABLE rule or SSOT — never evict

scoring_policy.one_shot_rule_text: If a context item is the sole relevant item for the current task, its score receives the invoke bonus from its invoke_role.

function.equation: score = sum_i(w_i * x_i), x_i in [0,1], weights normalized by sum, score clamped to [0,1]

sim.description: Semantic similarity to current audit task

unknown_date_recency.rationale: Unknown date treated as medium recency

continuity.description: Session continuity -- items used in prior turns score higher

phase_1.description: Evict working-tier items below score threshold

phase_5.description: Emergency: truncate anchor items -- mark as degraded

cache_policy.note: Cache is advisory only. A cache miss is not an error.

compatibility_rules.patch: additive-only field additions, no removal, no semantic change
compatibility_rules.minor: new optional fields, deprecations, non-breaking semantic clarifications
compatibility_rules.major: breaking field changes, removal, scoring model changes

migration_strategy: validate existing archive against new schema

acceptance_criteria: IMMUTABLE rules loaded and active before every audit session
Audit output passes all 19 self-validation checks
Score within +/-10 points of identical audit on different LLM (cross-LLM consistency target)
non_goals: Replacement of technical code audit (STEM-AI is trust classification, not code inspection)
Regulatory determination (output is LLM-generated, not a clinical certification)
Individual author profiling or competitive intelligence

provenance_registry.note: Primary canonical specification. SHA256 updated from active repository state on 2026-04-27.
Universal skill entry point. SHA256 updated from active repository state on 2026-04-27.

10-repo live audit batch (March 2026): 8 T0, 1 T1, 1 T2, 0 T3/T4. External result batch is referenced here, but its source artifact is not embedded in this repository, so SHA256 remains a placeholder.

semantic_validation_policy.json_schema_limits_note: Cross-field SLO ordering constraints cannot be enforced by JSON Schema alone.

rules.note: STEM-AI has exactly 18 IMMUTABLE rules. Fewer indicates corruption or unauthorized removal.

required_equalities: mica_schema_version must match schema $id version component

doc_sanity_checks.note: design_invariants count must equal 18 (STEM-AI IMMUTABLE rule count). Mismatch = unauthorized modification or version drift.

loading_order: 1. Load memory/mica.yaml -- verify package structure and mode
2. Load this archive -- read canonical_statement and design_invariants (18 IMMUTABLE rules)
3. Load memory/stem-ai-playbook.v1.1.2.md -- session protocol and rubric drift guard
4. Load spec/STEM-AI_v1.1.2_CORE.md -- canonical rubric and execution instruction
5. Load discrimination/ files on demand during audit stages
6. Run PCT self-tests to confirm MICA integrity before starting audit

session_report_format.format_template: [MICA READY] stem-ai-bio v1.1.2 | mode: protocol_evolution | invariants: 18 active | Gate: {gate} | Track: {track}

operation_meta.objective: Machine-checkable governance layer for STEM-AI protocol evolution. Encodes 18 IMMUTABLE rubric rules as design_invariants, 10 failure modes as lessons, and empirical validation batch as provenance.
operation_meta.invocation_notes: Primary invocation via SKILL.md (readme_protocol). MICA layer initializes before audit execution. 18 IMMUTABLE rules are anchor-pinned and never evicted.
